title: Aluminum Breaks $3,000 for First Time in Three Years as Supply Crisis Deepens
summary: Aluminum prices have surpassed $3,000 per ton for the first time since March 2022, marking an 18% rally in 2025 due to supply disruptions and China's production cap. LME aluminum traded near $2,996/ton on December 31, 2025, amid smelter closures and a growing global deficit. U.S. producers like Century Aluminum saw significant share gains, with analysts forecasting deficits up to 600,000 tons in 2026.

title: Aluminum fell to 3,420 USD/T on March 13, 2026
summary: Aluminum prices dropped to 3,420 USD/T on March 13, 2026, down 2.82% from the previous day, despite a 12.30% monthly rise and 27.52% yearly increase. The price reflects tightening market conditions, with Rio Tinto offering premiums to Japanese buyers. Forecasts predict trading at 3437.81 USD/T by quarter-end and 3616.04 in 12 months.

title: LME Aluminium Price Surge Hits $3,269/Tonne in March 2026
summary: LME aluminum prices reached $3,269/tonne in March 2026, driven by inventory drawdowns and ongoing supply constraints. The surge highlights persistent market tightness amid global production challenges.

title: Aluminum Prices Surge Following New 50% Tariff: What You Need to Know
summary: Aluminum prices are rising again after U.S. tariffs on imported extrusions increased to 50% in June 2025, impacting Midwest Transaction Price, Premium, and LME benchmarks. The escalation has caused supply chain disruptions and higher costs. Market charts updated as of March 13, 2026, show marked increases across key indicators.

title: Aluminium slides as Trump vows quick Mideast war resolution
summary: Aluminum prices slipped 2.88% to 24,465 yuan ($3,550.95) per ton on the Shanghai Futures Exchange after President Trump's pledge eased Middle East supply fears. London contracts also dropped as investors took profits. Analysts warn of vulnerability to renewed surges from regional shocks.

title: Aluminum prices surge to a four-year high amid war
summary: Middle East tensions drove aluminum prices to a near four-year high of about $3,540 per ton on the London Metal Exchange, the highest since March 2022. Prices rose nearly 10% over the past week due to supply concerns from key producing regions. The video discusses ongoing market volatility from geopolitical risks.

title: Aluminum Breaks $3,000 for First Time in Three Years as Supply Crisis Looms
summary: South32 will place its Mozal smelter in Mozambique under care and maintenance today due to failure to secure affordable electricity, removing 240,000 tons of annual aluminum production capacity. Analysts forecast a global aluminum deficit of 200,000 tons in 2026, potentially widening to 600,000 tons if the closure is permanent. Supply constraints from China's capacity cap, Western energy issues, and strong demand from electrification support higher prices.

title: Supply fears lift LME aluminium to nearly four-year high
summary: Escalating Middle East conflict has driven LME aluminum prices to a nearly four-year high of $3,544 per ton amid fears of supply disruptions through the Strait of Hormuz, which affects 9% of global production. Recent shutdowns at Qatalum in Qatar and force majeure at Aluminium Bahrain exacerbate concerns. Analysts warn prolonged disruptions could tighten global supply significantly.

title: Aluminium reaches four-year highs due to Middle East shipping disruptions

title: IBAAS–2026 to Spotlight Innovation, Sustainability and Digital Transformation in the Global Aluminium Industry

title: Rio Tinto, Prysmian Team Up for Green Data Center Aluminum
summary: Rio Tinto and Prysmian are trialing low-carbon aluminium cables for data centres using hydropowered smelter aluminium and ELYSIS technology, which eliminates direct GHG emissions by producing oxygen instead. summary: US copper prices are expected to remain volatile in 2026 due to uncertainties in Chinese demand, supply constraints, and high inventories in CME warehouses reaching record levels over 453,000 tonnes. title: FLASH: ESDM estimates Indonesia's nickel production to fall by up to 15% in 2026
summary: The Indonesian Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources (ESDM) plans to lower the 2026 nickel ore production target to 250-260 million tonnes, a 10-15% decrease from 2025 levels, to balance global supply-demand. This adjustment aligns with domestic smelter capacities and supports nickel prices, which recently reached US$18,000 per tonne. The government remains open to imports from the Philippines at current levels of 10-15 million tonnes per year.

title: Indonesia Shakes Global Nickel Market: World's Largest Mine Sees 70% Production Cut in Push to Inflate Prices
summary: Indonesia's ESDM has slashed PT Weda Bay Nickel's 2026 production quota to 12 million wet metric tonnes, a 71% cut from 42 million in 2025, impacting global nickel supply. This triggered a 2% surge in LME nickel prices to nearly $18,000 per tonne, shifting market dynamics from surplus to potential shortage. Smelters may need to import ore from the Philippines if the quota holds.

title: Nickel prices hit 15-month high on robust sentiment
summary: Indonesia may cut its 2026 nickel ore RKAB to 250 million wet metric tonnes from 379 million in 2025, despite expected consumption rising 11% to 330 million wmt. This potential reduction contributes to nickel prices hitting a 15-month high amid robust market sentiment. The move highlights supply constraints from the world's top producer.

title: Nickel Mining Industry Report 2026: Production by Country, Prices, Companies, Major Operating Mines, Competitive Landscape, Major Exploration and Development Projects, 2025-2035
summary: Global nickel production is forecast to rise 9.4% to 4.4 million tonnes in 2026, driven mainly by Indonesia despite recent quota cuts, with smaller growth from Canada, Philippines, New Caledonia, and Brazil. title: Nickel Prices Hit $18,000 in 2026 Amid Global Oversupply, US Boosts Domestic Supply Chain

title: Copper Cables: Antitrust Launches Investigation into Alleged Cartel ... Prysmian and Nexans Stock Market Drops
summary: Italy's Antitrust Authority has launched an investigation into major low-voltage copper cable manufacturers, including Prysmian Cavi e Sistemi Italia Srl and others, for alleged price-fixing since 2005.
